title: Repeat offender — second or subsequent violation
trigger: Same user triggers shadow AI detection after a prior warning has been documented. Identified when SOC analyst enriches a new alert and finds a prior case in the AI Governance Risk Register for the same user within 90 days.

immediate.action: Confirm repeat status: query the AI Governance Risk Register for prior cases involving this user. Retrieve prior case ID, date, and outcome.
Document the repeat violation with full context: prior warning date, current detection details, time elapsed since prior warning, and whether the same or different AI service is involved.
Escalate to the user's direct manager and HR Business Partner simultaneously. Use comms/individual-warning-repeat template.

short_term.action: Initiate formal disciplinary documentation per HR policy. Conduct formal meeting with the employee. Document the meeting outcome and any commitments.
Assess whether access restriction is warranted. Options: enhanced DLP monitoring, conditional-access policy restricting AI domains for the user, or device-level browser policy changes.
If access restriction is applied: document the restriction, set a review date (30 days), and notify the user and manager of the restriction scope and duration.

long_term.action: Review enforcement effectiveness. If repeat offender rate exceeds 10%, escalate to the AI Governance Committee for program-level review of comms clarity, approved-tool availability, and policy enforceability.
Update policy if patterns indicate systemic issues (e.g., lack of approved alternatives, unclear AUP language, insufficient training). Adjust comms cadence for affected business units.

decision_tree.node: Is this the second or a subsequent violation?
Was the prior warning issued within the last 90 days?
decision_tree.no: Treat as soft repeat; re-issue warning with manager CC.

decision_tree.node: Is there evidence of sensitive data involvement in this occurrence?
decision_tree.yes: Escalate to RB-002 in parallel with disciplinary process.

policy_references: AUP § 3 — Use of external AI services
metrics_to_capture: Time from detection to escalation (target: ≤ 4 hours)
Time from escalation to formal HR meeting (target: ≤ 10 business days)
Repeat offender rate across the program (target: < 10% within 90 days)
Percentage of repeat cases resulting in access restriction

references.title: NIST AI RMF — MANAGE-4 Risk response
